details: Demand history should be dense daily grain (date x warehouse x product).
Processed daily metrics should preserve full transactional row coverage.
SKU risk table should be one row per product-warehouse (single supplier per SKU master).

Critical column null check for product_id, category, unit_cost, unit_price, supplier_id.
Critical column null check for supplier_id, reliability_score, average_lead_time_days.
Critical column null check for date, warehouse_id, product_id, units_demanded, units_fulfilled, fill_rate.
Critical column null check for product_id, warehouse_id, supplier_id, service_risk_score, stockout_risk_score, governance_priority_score, risk_tier.
No negative units or values allowed in operational raw tables.
Derived fill rate must stay within [0,1].
Units fulfilled + lost sales must equal units demanded.
Stockout flag must align to lost sales > 0.
lost_sales_revenue should reconcile to units_lost_sales * unit_price within rounding tolerance.
Inventory value should reconcile to on-hand units * unit cost.
available_units should equal on_hand_units - reserved_units.
Recomputed trapped WC proxy should match impact summary.
Recomputed excess inventory proxy should match impact summary.
Supplier delay impact proxy should reconcile to weighted delay factor formula.
Aggregated impact totals should reconcile to overall summary.

Aggregated impact totals should reconcile to overall summary.

12M opportunity proxy should be non-negative for a valid value pool.
No fulfilled or lost units allowed when demand denominator is zero.
Processed table denominator sanity should match raw logic.
Governance score must reconcile to declared weighted formula.
risk_tier must align with governance_priority_score thresholds.
main_risk_driver should match max risk component.
Non-low risk tiers must map to active intervention actions.
Top governance queue should remain reasonably stable under small weighting perturbations.
All required executive chart PNG files should exist.
Very small PNG files may indicate rendering issues.
Required curated release tables (contracts, scoring, impact, dashboard, and SQL gate) must exist.
Data contract validation must have zero FAIL checks.
Contract warnings should remain zero for release quality discipline.
Optional run-log from one-command orchestration should exist for production-style traceability.
Dashboard embedded fact should reconcile to processed daily totals.
Dashboard must contain required filters, charts, and drilldown table structure.
Executive-facing dashboard should not expose technical build metadata in the visible layout.
Avoiding absolute positioning reduces overlap risk across responsive screen widths.
Dashboard must retain explicit responsive layout rules for mobile/tablet/desktop safety.
Large HTML payloads degrade browser reliability and increase silent rendering failures.
Critical risk-scoring logic must remain in governed backend datasets, not browser-side logic.
Dashboard official snapshot file must exist and reconcile to KPI baseline metrics.
Executive summary fill-rate narrative must reconcile to KPI baseline output.
Executive summary stockout-rate narrative must reconcile to KPI baseline output.
Executive summary lost-sales statement should stay aligned (allowing rounded million formatting).
Heuristic check for causal overclaiming without proxy/assumption qualifiers.
